Article Title: Times Internet Half Marathon 2026: Four women, aged 11 to 52, redefine what it means to run
Article Text:
-

Shalu

Winner

Chip time: 00:20:26.30

"Chai kitni bhi age ho jaye, mujhe running nahin chhodna hai."

-

Bhavya Singh

,

1st Runner up

Chip time: 00:23:57.19

"It was an amazing experience. I really enjoyed the Times Internet Marathon."

-

Panchhi Singh

,

2nd Runner up

Chip time: 00:23:57:74

"I had a thrilling time. I was very happy with the event."

-

The Times Internet Half Marathon was never just a race. Across three extraordinary days starting with the expo on March 27th and 28th, building to the electrifying race day on March 29th over 15,000 runners filled the city with joy, sweat, and stories worth telling. In the 5K Female category, four women stood out. Not just for where they finished, but for who they are and what brought them to that starting line.Shalu was always fast.Her school knew it, her friends knew it, and deep down, she knew it too. She spent her school years running freely in the fields, becoming known for her speed. But when it came to competitions, her family held her back, she was too young, they said. So she waited. The moment she stepped into college, she stepped onto the national stage. First year of college, first national competition. That is the kind of athlete Shalu is.No matter how old she gets, she will never leave running. Her goals are clear: win a national medal, support her parents, and one day, stand at the Olympics.Bhavya Singh is 12 years old and already a medal-winning athlete.A professional skater since the age of 5, she has been collecting medals at CBSE Skating Championships and only recently expanded her world to include athletics. This was her very first marathon and she finished on the podium. Supported every step of the way by her father, Bhavya is the kind of young talent that makes you stop and pay attention.From the skating rink to the race track , Bhavya is building a career in sport one milestone at a time, and this podium finish is only her beginning.Just 11 years old, Panchhi Singh is already on her fifth marathon. She has been skating for a year, takes dedicated running coaching, and has a dream that is crystal clear she wants to become a sprint runner and build her entire career in athletics. What makes her story even more remarkable? She finished just milliseconds behind Bhavya Singh. Two milliseconds separated bronze from silver. She took it in her stride literally.At 11, fifth marathon, a podium finish decided by a heartbeat Panchhi Singh is a name to remember.
Article Summary: The times internet half marathon took place on march 29th. Over 15,000 runners took part in the race. In the 5k female category, four women stood out. Bhavya singh, panchhi singh, shalu and anuja were all on the podium.

Article Title: Speed, grit & the 5K surge: Inside the fastest race at Times Internet Half Marathon 2026
Article Text:
Times Internet Half Marathon 2026

The faces behind the pace

Vivek Chaudary

Vivek Chaudary

Harsh Mallik

Harsh Malik

Shivam: Running with purpose

Shivam

The nature of the 5K battle

Immediate acceleration

Tactical clarity

Perfectly timed execution

The final word

The city didn’t just wake up - it accelerated.At the Times Internet Half Marathon 2026, over 15,000 runners took over one of Delhi’s most iconic stretches - starting from the historic India Gate, sweeping through the grand, tree-lined avenues of Lutyens’ Delhi, and looping across wide, uninterrupted roads designed for pace and precision.It was a route that balanced beauty with performance - flat, fast, and unforgiving. Every turn carried momentum, every stretch demanded rhythm, and every kilometre pushed runners deeper into their limits, all while the capital stood on the sidelines, cheering them forward.Within this electric setting, the 5K category emerged as one of the most intense races of the day.Short in distance, ruthless in execution - the 5K left no room for hesitation.Vivek’s journey into competitive running began after the 12th grade - but what followed was a steady and determined rise.From discovering the sport to representing Himachal Pradesh at the national level, his path reflects discipline built over time. He is not a product of overnight success, but of consistent effort - an athlete who earned his place on bigger stages through persistence and focus.Harsh brings with him the kind of experience that only time and consistency can build.His journey has been gradual, shaped by patience and learning. Starting at his own pace, he has evolved into a runner who understands the demands of racing - when to hold back, when to push, and how to stay composed under pressure.In a race as sharp as the 5K, that awareness becomes a defining strength.Shivam’s journey is not just about performance - it is about evolution.Built on the foundations of school athletics and sharpened through years of competitive exposure, he has grown into an athlete who understands both the science and the soul of running. His presence reflects discipline, resilience, and quiet confidence.Having competed across national platforms, his journey carries both experience and ambition. There is a certain composure in the way he runs - measured, aware, and deeply intentional.Because for Shivam, running isn’t just about pace.It’s about purpose.The 5K is unforgiving.There’s no settling in, no gradual rhythm to ease into. From the very first step, runners are forced into decisions - how fast to go, how long to hold, when to break.It becomes a race defined by:Because over this distance, even the smallest hesitation can cost everything.At the Times Internet Half Marathon 2026, the 5K was more than just a race - it was a test of instinct.From Vivek’s steady rise to Harsh’s composed experience, and Shivam’s purposeful stride, the category brought together different journeys on a single course.What united them was simple:The will to push harder.The discipline to stay focused.And the courage to go all in.Because in the 5K, there are no second chances.Only the run.
Article Summary: Vivek chaudary, harsh mallik and shivam malik represent himachal pradesh at the times internet half marathon 2026. Over 15,000 runners took over one of delhi’s most iconic stretches - sweeping through the grand, tree-lined avenues of lutyens’ delhi.

Article Title: Kirk Cousins: Kirk Cousins joins Las Vegas Raiders as Klint Kubiak makes bold veteran call before rookie quarterback plan unfolds
Article Text:
Kirk Cousins (Image Via Getty)

Kirk Cousins and Klint Kubiak reunion gives Las Vegas Raiders a steady plan at quarterback

The Las Vegas Raiders have made a clear move before the new season. They have agreed to sign Kirk Cousins, as confirmed by his agent on Thursday. The deal details are not out yet, but the message is simple. The team wants an experienced quarterback in the room right now.This comes just two days after new head coach Klint Kubiak openly said he wanted a veteran QB. The Raiders are also expected to pick a young quarterback in the upcoming draft. So this move is about balance. They want someone steady now, while preparing for the future at the same time.For Kirk Cousins, this is a fresh start after leaving the Atlanta Falcons. His time there had good moments, but it also came with injuries and tough phases. Now, he walks into a situation where he is clearly needed.The Raiders had only Aidan O’Connell on their roster after trading Geno Smith to the New York Jets. So adding Cousins fills a big gap right away.There is also a familiar face waiting for him. Cousins and Klint Kubiak worked together earlier with the Minnesota Vikings. During that time, Cousins played some of his best football.He threw for over 12,000 yards and 94 touchdowns in three seasons. In 2021, he even made the Pro Bowl.Kubiak has been very clear about his plan. He said, “Ideally, you don’t want him to start day one. You’d love him to be able to learn behind somebody. That’s in a perfect world.” That line explains everything. The Raiders are likely to draft a young QB, possibly Fernando Mendoza, but they don’t want to rush him.Cousins fits that role perfectly. He brings experience, calmness, and a clear way of running the offense. A young player can learn by just watching him every day.Looking back, Cousins has had a long career. He started with Washington after being picked in 2012. Over 14 seasons, he has thrown for 44,700 yards and 298 touchdowns. Even last season, he showed he can still contribute. He stepped in late and helped his team finish strong.So this move is not just about one season. It’s about building the right setup. The Raiders now have a bridge between present and future, and Cousins is right in the middle of it.
Article Summary: Kirk cousins and klint kubiak reunion gives las vegas raiders a steady plan at quarterback. The raiders are also expected to pick a young quarterback in the upcoming draft. This move is about balance. They want someone steady now, while preparing for the future at the same time.

Article Title: Sameer Rizvi's masterclass: Delhi Capitals trump Mumbai Indians on tricky Kotla pitch
Article Text:
Sameer Rizvi (Image credit: BCCI/IPL)

Watch Sameer Rizvi on batting approach: simple mindset, big impact for DC Sameer Rizvi on batting approach: simple mindset, big impact for DC

NEW DELHI: Sameer Rizvi has finally arrived on the big stage, two years after being identified by IPL scouts.On Saturday evening, on a Kotla pitch that sucked the life out of the match, he injected adrenaline with a commanding 51-ball 90 as Delhi Capitals cruised past the 163-run target set by Mumbai Indians with 11 balls and six wickets to spare to go to the top of the standings. The 22-year-old Rizwan has now played the lead role in two consecutive tricky chases following his impressive performance against LSG earlier.The afternoon started with the Delhi crowd going berserk after seeing Rohit Sharma taking guard. Capitals have been yearning for slower pitches since the last season to aid their spin trio of Kuldeep Yadav, Axar Patel and Vipraj Nigam. Rohit brought used all his experience to keep the scoreboard moving, with frequent boundaries, even as runscoring looked tough on the dry pitch. The crowd roared with each boundary that Rohit hit till he was caught at extra-cover off Axar for 35 off 26 in the 10th over. Scoring became excruciatingly tough after Rohit’s dismissal even as Suryakumar Yadav grafted a 36-ball 51. The Capitals pacers relied on cutters while pegging away at a back of a length.MI’s 162/6 looked like a challenging score before Capitals opener Pathum Nissanka went hard at the top of the chase to set it up for Rizvi to eventually bat MI out of the game.MI skipper for the match Suryakumar Yadav, standing in for an unwell Hardik Pandya, was lost for answers during the counter-attack.The crowd remained flat for much of the match before Rizvi took over once Nissanka departed for 44 off 30 balls, with the scoreboard reading 73/3 in the 10th over. Rizvi, raised on the sluggish pitches of western UP, went on a rampage. In the next 6.3 overs, DC smashed 78 runs with David Miller’s only job feeding the strike to Rizvi.Rizvi, coming out to bat as an Impact Substitute, went about dismantling MI’s bowling with consummate ease. Rizvi brought shots that broke the rules of conventional batting. He knew the MI pacers would hit the hard lengths just like DC had done it. So, he cleared his front foot and went about slapping the ball through the off-side.Leg-spinner Mayank Markande looked completely out of his depth as Rizvi negated the turn by skipping down the track and smacking him down the ground. Rizvi’s approach of using his feet against the spinners was in sharp contrast to the MI batters, who were largely rooted to the crease. Surya’s only response to the Capitals spinners was the sweep for singles.Rizvi has now cracked the code in these short tricky chases. Like he did in Lucknow in the previous match, he doesn’t get edgy at the top of his innings. The string of dot balls doesn’t perturb him. He waits to find his rhythm on these pitches. He respects the established international bowlers, like he did against Jasprit Bumrah and Mitchell Santner. Just like he pounced on Shahbaz Nadeem’s innocuous left-arm spin against Lucknow Super Giants, he devoured Markande on Saturday evening.The knock clearly left the MI camp shaken. Surya’s captaincy looked unimpressive. Santner didn’t complete his quota of overs and Bumrah came back in the attack a bit late.
Article Summary: Delhi capitals beat mumbai indians by six wickets. Sameer rizvi scored 90 off 51 balls as dc cruised past 163-run target. The 22-year-old has now played the lead role in two consecutive tricky chases.

Article Title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Yashasvi Jaiswal script IPL history, become fastest Indian pair to...
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Yashasvi Jaiswal (BCCI Photo)

Watch ‘Bowlers have it hard’ – Glenn Phillips breaks down modern T20 cricket ‘Bowlers have it hard’ – Glenn Phillips breaks down modern T20 cricket

NEW DELHI: Rajasthan Royals ’ young opening pair, Yashasvi Jaiswal and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, created history during their IPL clash against Gujarat Titans with a record-breaking partnership milestone on Saturday.The duo, already in great form, needed just a couple of deliveries to achieve something special and didn’t disappoint.Jaiswal and Sooryavanshi became the fastest Indian pair to reach 500 partnership runs in IPL history in terms of balls faced.Before the match, they were just three runs short, sitting at 497 runs off 246 balls. It took them only two more deliveries to cross the 500-run mark, setting a new benchmark.They broke the previous record held by Virender Sehwag and Gautam Gambhir, who had reached the same milestone in 309 balls.This achievement highlighted the aggressive and consistent approach of the Rajasthan openers this season.Earlier, Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag won the toss and chose to bat first.Gujarat Titans were without their regular skipper Shubman Gill, who missed the game due to a muscle spasm, with Rashid Khan stepping in as captain. Kumar Kushagra came in as Gill’s replacement.Rajasthan made two changes to their lineup, bringing in Tushar Deshpande and Shubham Dubey.Both teams also named their impact substitutes ahead of the match.With a strong opening pair firing and records being broken, Rajasthan looked confident, while Gujarat had the added challenge of coping without their key player.Yashasvi Jaiswal, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Dhruv Jurel(w), Riyan Parag(c), Shimron Hetmyer, Donovan Ferreira, Ravindra Jadeja, Jofra Archer, Nandre Burger, Tushar Deshpande, Sandeep SharmaRavi Bishnoi, Lhuan-dre Pretorius, Shubham Dubey, Brijesh Sharma, Ravi SinghKumar Kushagra, Sai Sudharsan, Jos Buttler(w), Glenn Phillips, Washington Sundar, Rahul Tewatia, Rashid Khan(c), Kagiso Rabada, Ashok Sharma, Mohammed Siraj, Prasidh KrishnaShahrukh Khan, Jason Holder, Manav Suthar, Anuj Rawat, Jayant Yadav
Article Summary: Yashasvi jaiswal and vaibhav sooryavanshi became the fastest indian pair to reach 500 partnership runs in ipl history in terms of balls faced. Rajasthan royals beat gujarat titans by eight wickets in their ipl clash.

Article Title: IPL 2026: Priyansh Arya, Ayush Mhatre lead the GenZ charge
Article Text:
Priyansh Arya and Ayush Mhatre (BCCI/IPL Photo)

Watch Shashank Singh on Punjab’s strong start, Priyansh’s growth & team mentality | IPL 2026 Shashank Singh on Punjab’s strong start, Priyansh’s growth & team mentality | IPL 2026

Arya’s mental toughness aids PBKS

CHENNAI : The new generation of top-order batters, with their audacious approach, have lately been hogging the limelight in the IPL. While 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has emerged as the poster boy of this Gen Z crop, he is not a lone ranger. Two more young willow wielders are coming into the frame as serious contenders for bigger things in future.During Punjab Kings’ fivewicket win over Chennai Super Kings at the MA Chidambaram Stadium here on Friday, it was the tale of two young guns — Priyansh Arya and Ayush Mhatre. While the 18-year-old Mhatre, with his clever strokeplay, powered CSK to 209 with a 43-ball 73, the 24-year-old Arya launched PBKS’s chase in style. The opener’s whirlwind 39 off just 11 balls, at a staggering strike rate of 354.5, took the game out of CSK’s hands early. Both made their debut last year and enjoyed breakout seasons. Now, into their second IPL campaign, they’ve begun brightly and enough to catch the eye of former India spinner Ravichandran Ashwin, who heaped praise on the “Gen Z players who put on a batting festivity”.“Both of them (Arya and Mhatre) batted brilliantly. The way these‘Gen Z’ batters played, they are changing our mindset. And I absolutely enjoyed seeing their batting.I also expect a lot more ‘Gen Z’ bowlers, I want more new bowlers to come up,” Ashwin said.Ashwin, a former CSK stalwart, felt No.3 batter Mhatre’s knock and the shots he played was the silver lining in the five-time champions’ defeat to PBKS, amid a season already strewn with early struggles for CSK.“The way Mhatre batted, this should be the happiest news and a big positive for CSK. For the next 10 years, they have found a champion player. CSK didn’t get him in the auction, they signed him as an injury replacement. He has shown what he can do, the way he played and pulled Marco Jansen for a boundary, it was amazing,” Ashwin added.During his onslaught against CSK, Arya operated with the freedom of knowing there was batting depth to back him up. Hence, he was swinging at everything that came his way with fearless intent and even perished while trying to hit big.Talking about his young teammate, Shashank Singh said it’s Arya’s “strong mentality” that sets him apart. “With Priyansh what we, as a team, really liked last year is that he’s mentally very tough. He knows his role. He knows the areas in which he can score runs. He’s very young but the way he’s matured regarding his batting is fantastic,” said Shashank.
Article Summary: Priyansh arya and ayush mhatre both made their debut last year and enjoyed breakout seasons. Arya launched pbks’s chase in style with a whirlwind 39 off just 11 balls, at a staggering strike rate of 354.5. Ravichandran ashwin heaped praise on the ‘gen z players who put on a batting festivity’

NEW DELHI: Delhi Capitals’ fragile top order will be under scrutiny when they take on Mumbai Indians in their first home game of Indian Premier League 2026 on Saturday.Despite hopes of stability, DC’s batting woes persisted in their opener against Lucknow Super Giants, where they slumped to 26 for four while chasing 142. KL Rahul fell for a duck, with Nitish Rana and Pathum Nissanka also departing early. However, a composed unbeaten fifty from youngster Sameer Rizvi, alongside Tristan Stubbs, rescued DC with a six-wicket win.While Rizvi’s emergence is a positive, DC will need more consistency at the top, especially against a potent Mumbai attack led by Jasprit Bumrah. Options like Abishek Porel and Prithvi Shaw remain under consideration.DC’s bowling, though, looked solid. T Natarajan starred with three wickets, while Lungi Ngidi and Mukesh Kumar were effective. The spin duo of Kuldeep Yadav and skipper Axar Patel controlled the middle overs.Mumbai, meanwhile, arrive high on confidence after a record chase against Kolkata Knight Riders. Rohit Sharma (78) and Ryan Rickelton (81) led the charge, while Shardul Thakur impressed with the ball. Fitness concerns around Suryakumar Yadav could, however, be a factor.With this being the first day game of the season and a thunderstorm forecast for the afternoon, conditions could play a key role. The square boundaries are fairly even, with one side slightly shorter, while the straight boundary is comparatively longer — factors that still point towards a potentially high-scoring contest.The pitch itself is expected to offer minimal assistance to spinners, with noticeably less turn than usual. Early in the game, the surface could be a bit tacky, especially with the weather in play, which may aid the fast bowlers with some movement before it settles down for the batters.KL Rahul (wk), Pathum Nissanka, Nitish Rana, Axar Patel (c), Tristan Stubbs, David Miller, Vipraj Nigam, Lungi Ngidi, Kuldeep Yadav, T Natarajan, Mukesh KumarImpact Sub: Sameer RizviRohit Sharma, Ryan Rickelton (wk), Tilak Varma, Suryakumar Yadav, Hardik Pandya (c), Sherfane Rutherford, Shardul Thakur, Mayank Markande, AM Ghazanfar, Trent Boult, Jasprit BumrahImpact Sub: Naman DhirConditions are expected to start with some sunshine before turning increasingly cloudy, with thunderstorms likely in parts of the area during the afternoon. Air quality is also a concern, expected to remain at very unhealthy levels, while the temperature will hover around 30°C.As the match progresses, cloud cover is likely to persist with intermittent thunderstorms in nearby areas. Rain chances are set to rise steadily — from around 18% at the time of the toss to nearly 50% by early evening — which could have a significant impact on proceedings.The DC vs MI clash will be held at the Arun Jaitley Stadium in Delhi, with the match set to begin at 3:30 PM IST. The game will be broadcast on the Star Sports Network, while live streaming will be available on JioHotstar.Abishek Porel, KL Rahul, Nitish Rana, Sameer Rizvi, Tristan Stubbs, Axar Patel, Ashutosh Sharma, Dushmantha Chameera, Kuldeep Yadav, Mukesh Kumar, Vipraj Nigam, Mitchell Starc, Tripurana Vijay, David Miller, Auqib Nabi Dar, Pathum Nissanka, Lungi Ngidi, Prithvi Shaw, Kyle Jamieson, Ajay Jadav Mandal, T Natarajan, Madhav Tiwari, Karun Nair, Sahil Parakh.Hardik Pandya (c), Quinton de Kock (wk), Danish Malewar, Robin Minz (wk), Ryan Rickelton (wk), Sherfane Rutherford, Rohit Sharma, Suryakumar Yadav, Atharva Ankolekar, Raj Bawa, Corbin Bosch, Will Jacks, Mayank Rawat, Naman Dhir, Mitchell Santner, Shardul Thakur, Tilak Varma, Ashwani Kumar, Trent Boult, Jasprit Bumrah, Deepak Chahar, AM Ghazanfar, Mayank Markande, Mohammad Izhar, Raghu Sharma.
Article Summary: Delhi capitals take on mumbai indians in their first home game of indian premier league 2026 on saturday. Dc's batting woes persisted in their opener against lucknow super giants, where they slumped to 26 for four while chasing 142. Rohit sharma (78) and ryan rickelton (81) led the charge against kolkata knight riders.

Article Title: Will Shreyas Iyer play today? PBKS sweat over captain’s fitness ahead of CSK clash
Article Text:
Punjab Kings' captain Shreyas Iyer winces in pain after getting hit by a shot from batting partner Cooper Connolly (AP Photo/Ashwini Bhatia)

Watch Greenstone Lobo predicts IPL 2026 winner Greenstone Lobo predicts IPL 2026 winner

There is cautious optimism around Shreyas Iyer ’s availability as Punjab Kings prepare to face Chennai Super Kings in their IPL 2026 clash at the MA Chidambaram Stadium.Iyer suffered a painful blow to his wrist in the previous match after being struck by a powerful shot from Cooper Connolly at the non-striker’s end, raising immediate concerns over his participation in this high-profile encounter.However, early signs suggest PBKS could receive a major boost. Assistant coach Brad Haddin confirmed that the injury is not serious, easing fears within the camp."There is no break (in the bone), which is a good thing. Now it is just about getting the swelling down and getting the movement back in the hand, so he should be right for tomorrow," Haddin said.The update has come as a relief for Punjab , especially with Iyer’s leadership and batting crucial to their plans this season. The skipper has also reportedly looked in good touch during practice, even clearing the ropes with ease, indicating that his recovery is on track.PBKS head into the contest with momentum after a strong start to their campaign, including a win over Gujarat Titans. Their recent record at Chepauk further boosts confidence, having won at the venue in each of the last three seasons — a rare feat against CSK at home.On the other hand, Chennai Super Kings find themselves under early pressure following a disappointing batting display in their opener against Rajasthan Royals. With conditions in Chennai expected to be more batting-friendly, the contest could turn into a high-scoring affair.
Article Summary: Shreyas iyer suffered a painful blow to his wrist in the previous match. Assistant coach brad haddin confirmed that the injury is not serious. The skipper has also reportedly looked in good touch during practice. Pbks head into the contest with momentum after a strong start to their campaign.

Article Title: WWE SmackDown Results & Highlights 3-april-2026: Randy Orton's mystery caller revealed, Lil Hatchy appears with Trick Williams, and more
Article Text:
Image via WWE

What happened in the opening segment of WWE SmackDown?

Rhea Ripley vs. Michin

Uncle Howdy vs. Tama Tonga



Aleister Black vs. Matt Cardona

Charlotte Flair & Alexa Bliss vs. Bayley & Lyra Valkyria

R-Truth & Damian Priest vs. The Miz & Kit Wilson

Sami Zayn vs. Carmelo Hayes



WWE SmackDown aired its April 3 episode from the Enterprise Center in St. Louis, Missouri. The show opened with a shocking return and heel turn, building major excitement for the rest of the show. Following that, the event saw some highly interesting matches and segments. The main event saw a major rematch for the US title between Carmelo Hayes and Sami Zayn, which saw an unexpected end caused by Trick Williams and his new ally, popular rapper Lil Hatchy.Randy Orton came out in the opening segment of the show. As he was addressing his hometown audience, Cody Rhodes stormed down to the ring to brawl with him. Suddenly, Pat McAfee returned and entered the ring to attack Rhodes in a major shocker. He took shots at WWE and blamed Rhodes for SmackDown's recent low viewership and low WrestleMania ticket sales. The duo later walked out together.After defeating B-Fab last week, Rhea Ripley squared off with Jade Cargill's other ally, Michin. The duo had a good match that ended with Ripley tapping Michin out of the Prism Lock. Following that, she was once again attacked by the trio, only for her old ally, IYO Sky from RAW, to come to her aid.This was a singles match between Uncle Howdy and Tama Tonga. The back-and-forth contest between the duo saw Solo Sikoa coming down the aisle in the final moments. After he and Tonga had an argument, Howdy took advantage and hit Sister Abigail for the win, then took his lantern back.The singles match between Aleister Black and Matt Cardona was a fast-paced showdown. The match ended with Black countering Cardona and hitting the Black Mass to seal the win.Both teams squared off in a tag team collision. The match was an evenly matched showdown between four of the top competitors in the women's division. However, it ended with Charlotte Flair and Alexa Bliss hitting the double Natural Selection for the win. Following that, Nia Jax and Lash Legend came out to attack both teams.R-Truth and Damian Priest defended the WWE Tag Team Championships against The Miz and Kit Wilson. The match was captivating, with Danhausen coming down to the ring. He asked for a tag, and Truth did it. Danhausen then got in the face of The Miz and tried to curse him, only for the referee to come in between them. This led the referee to hurt his right arm as he was counting the three-count for The Miz. Following that, R-Truth took him down, and the referee counted the fall with his left arm as Danhausen escaped The Awesome One.This was a rematch for the WWE United States title from last week between Sami Zayn and Carmelo Hayes. Trick Williams was at the ringside for the match. The match was the highlight of the night, captivating the audience throughout. The bout finally came to an end when Melo was favoring his injured leg, and Zayn hit him with the Helluva Kick to retain the match. After that, Lil Hatchy and Trick Williams attacked Zayn.Also read: WWE rumors suggest The Rock could make a surprise entry at WrestleMania 42 to help Randy Orton
Article Summary: Wwe smackdown aired its april 3 episode from the enterprise center in st. louis, missouri. The show opened with a shocking return and heel turn, building major excitement for the rest of the show. The main event saw a rematch for the us title between carmelo hayes and sami zayn.

Article Title: Hardik Pandya: IPL 2026, DC vs MI: Why is Hardik Pandya not playing vs Delhi Capitals?
Article Text:
Hardik Pandya (Image credit: BCCI/IPL)

DC vs MI : Why isn’t Hardik Pandya playing today?

Watch Sameer Rizvi on batting approach: simple mindset, big impact for DC Sameer Rizvi on batting approach: simple mindset, big impact for DC

Poll Do you think absence of Hardik Pandya will impact Mumbai Indians performance? Yes, it will significantly impact No, they will manage fine

Playing XIs:

Delhi Capitals (Playing XI):

Mumbai Indians (Playing XI):

Here are the Impact substitutes for both the teams:

NEW DELHI: Delhi Capitals captain Axar Patel won the toss and opted to field against five-time champions Mumbai Indians at the Arun Jaitley Stadium in Delhi on Saturday."I choose to bowl first. First match at home, I want to see how the wicket is behaving. It looks a bit dry, a bit hard. We chased well in the last match. To chase down the target from 26/ 4 in the powerplay, it gives a lot of confidence to the group. Even the top-order know that there are batters in the lower order who can step up if they fail. Playing a home game is always beneficial. Our DC supporters will cheer for us. Same team.," Axar said at the toss.Mumbai Indians suffered a major blow as their captain Hardik Pandya missed the clash against Delhi Capitals.In Pandya’s absence, Suryakumar Yadav led the side.Stand-in captain Suryakumar Yadav explained the reason and said Pandya isn’t well."(News on Hardik) He's not well, doesn't look good, so he was not fit for today's game. So just getting into his shoes today. (On the win against KKR) If you win the first game after 13-14 years, there's a lot of positivity in the camp, and we're very happy, but completely different venue here from Wankhede.We have to take good responsibility and have a good game here," Surya said during the toss."(On the surface) Looks good, The toss was irrelevant. We wanted to bat first, they wanted to bowl first, but yeah, very happy to bat first. Looks good one. We've done here really well when we've batted first and defended. Quite a few changes. Deepak Chahar comes in for Hardik. Corbin Bosch comes in for Boulty, that's a tactical change. Boulty had a good game in the last game at Wankhede. And Mitch Santner comes in for Allah Ghazanfar," he said.Mumbai Indians also released a statement."Hardik Pandya is unwell and hence will not be taking the field today. Suryakumar Yadav, will be the captain today," Mumbai Indians said.KL Rahul(w), Pathum Nissanka, Nitish Rana, Axar Patel(c), Tristan Stubbs, David Miller, Vipraj Nigam, Lungi Ngidi, Kuldeep Yadav, T Natarajan, Mukesh KumarRohit Sharma, Ryan Rickelton(w), Tilak Varma, Suryakumar Yadav(c), Naman Dhir, Sherfane Rutherford, Mitchell Santner, Corbin Bosch, Shardul Thakur, Deepak Chahar, Jasprit BumrahDelhi Capitals Impact subs: Ashutosh Sharma, Auqib Nabi Dar, Sameer Rizvi, Dushmantha Chameera, Karun NairMumbai Indians Impact subs: Mayank Markande, Robin Minz, Raj Bawa, Trent Boult, Ashwani Kumar
Article Summary: Delhi capitals captain axar patel won the toss and opted to field against five-time champions mumbai indians. Mumbai indians suffered a major blow as their captain hardik pandya missed the clash against delhi capitals. Stand-in captain suryakumar yadav explained the reason and said pandya isn’t well.

Article Title: Yuvraj Singh on SRH’s Abhishek Sharma captaincy snub: ‘Now with what frame of mind will he play’?
Article Text:
Former India cricketer Yuvraj Singh, Abhishek Sharma's mentor, has stated that it is disappointing for any player who has given his ‘heart and soul’ to the franchise ‘ for years’ to have missed out on leading the side. SRH had announced Ishan Kishan as the stand-in skipper for IPL 2026, snubbing Abhishek and the decision had not gone down well with a section of the fans and even some cricket experts.

Watch Nitish Kumar Reddy breaks down SRH’s masterplan | post-match pc Nitish Kumar Reddy breaks down SRH’s masterplan | post-match pc

Poll Do you think Ishan Kishan is ready for the captaincy role in IPL? Yes, he has proven himself. No, he lacks experience as a captain.

One of the biggest talking points leading up to the start of IPL 2026 was the Sunrisers Hyderabad's choice of captain in the absence of regular skipper Pat Cummins. SRH announced Ishan Kishan as the stand-in skipper, snubbing Abhishek Sharma and the decision did not go down well with a section of the fans and even cricket experts. Now, former India cricketer Yuvraj Singh, Abhishek’s mentor, has stated that it is disappointing for any player who has given his ‘heart and soul’ to the franchise ‘ for years’ to have missed out on leading the side.“It is disappointing. I love Ishan Kishan. I have seen his growth as well in Indian cricket, his comeback as well,” Yuvraj Singh said in SportsTak Podcast. “If I give you an example, switch this from a player’s perspective - say if Abhishek Sharma, before the World Cup, came into the team, his state team won, scored a hundred in the final, came into the World Cup team, he scores runs and he is captain of the franchise.“On the other hand, Ishan Kishan, playing for one franchise for seven years, has performed, given his heart and soul for the franchise and also captains the state side as well, but does not get captaincy; he gets the vice-captaincy. Now what frame of mind he plays franchise cricket with - with a bit of disappointment.”However, Yuvraj Singh opined that this snub is something that Abhishek should take as motivation and they try to do even better than what he is doing currently."If it happened with me, I have to be clear, 'listen, this has happened, I am disappointed'. I spoke to Abhishek as well and told him we are going to put that disappointment aside and I will support my captain and I will try and do even better, because something has hurt me, ab aur zor lagaunga, to do better. That should be your motivation," said Yuvraj.While there has been official communication from SRH on the reason to pick Kishan over Abhishek, but the general feeling is that Ishan, who made a return to the Indian team after two years and has stellar World Cup campaign scoring 317 runs, had led his state side Jharkhand to the Syed Mushtaq Ali Trophy - India’s premier domestic T20 tournament - including a century in the final and 517 runs in the tournament to go with - was the ideal choice for the stop-gap arrangement.Further explaining Kishan's choice as skipper by SRH , Yuvraj looked back at his India career and the time when MS Dhoni was made the India captain in 2007 despite the presence of senior players. "For example, when I was in the Indian team, Harbhajan Singh, Virender Sehwag were seniors, I was vice-captain, but from nowhere MS Dhoni comes and becomes captain. Whatever the reasons maybe ... franchise cricket will not determine if you will captain India.You look at Shubman Gill; because everyone felt he is most hardworking, most sincere about the game, he thinks about cricket in a different way, and selectors felt this guy should captain India. Maybe same way Dhoni was made captain. And if someone is destined to captain, to do great things for India, that will happen.," said Yuvraj.Cummins, who led SRH in 2024 and 2025 is recovering from a back injury, and even though he has joined the SRH squad he is still unavailable for selection. The injury had ruled him out of the 2026 T20 World Cup. The Australian, though, has given his stamp of approval on Ishan’s elevation, stating, “Ishan had a really successful captaincy stint with his local side in the last year or so..’.
Article Summary: Srh announced ishan kishan as the stand-in skipper, snubbing abhishek sharma. The decision did not go down well with a section of the fans and even cricket experts. Yuvraj singh has stated that it is disappointing for any player who has given his ‘heart and soul’ to the franchise ‘ for years’ cummins, who led srh in 2024 and 2025 is recovering from a back injury, and even though he has joined the srh squad he is still unavailable for selection.

Article Title: Yuvraj Singh reveals what 'stood out' in Gautam Gambhir's coaching style
Article Text:
Gautam Gambhir and Yuvraj Singh

Watch Gautam Gambhir reflects on India’s World Cup win and backing Sanju Samson in the tournament Gautam Gambhir reflects on India’s World Cup win and backing Sanju Samson in the tournament

‘Sticking to his process led to World Cup triumph’

Poll Should other teams adopt Gambhir aggressive brand of cricket as a strategy? Yes, they should No, it’s too risky

NEW DELHI: Former India all-rounder Yuvraj Singh has credited head coach Gautam Gambhir ’s unwavering belief in his methods as the key reason behind India’s T20 World Cup triumph, saying the former opener’s clarity and courage under pressure made the difference.Speaking on Sports Tak, Yuvraj shed light on Gambhir’s coaching philosophy, describing him as someone with a sharp cricketing mind. “I have played with Gautam since under-16 and under-19, and I always felt he had a very good cricketing brain. The way he sees the game has always stood out,” Yuvraj said.He pointed to Gambhir’s credentials as both player and mentor, recalling his match-winning knocks in two World Cup finals. “He has performed at the highest level and played a major role in winning two World Cups. Then as a mentor with Kolkata Knight Riders, he won titles again. He must be doing something right,” Yuvraj added.Yuvraj emphasised that Gambhir’s biggest strength was his ability to stay committed to his gameplan despite criticism. “When you become India’s coach, there are too many eyes on you. One mistake is shown 100 times, one success 1000 times,” he said.Referring to the criticism Gambhir faced after India’s Test setbacks, Yuvraj noted: “He went through that phase but stuck to his process. That is what led India to the World Cup triumph. Now he is back on the high — there will always be ups and downs in cricket.”According to Yuvraj, Gambhir’s aggressive blueprint instilled fear in opponents. “He was clear — we have to play this brand of cricket even if there are failures. We are coming after you from the first ball. If it’s there to hit, players like Abhishek or Sanju will go for it from the first ball,” he explained.India’s bowling depth also played a crucial role, with Jasprit Bumrah leading the attack. “Whenever India are in trouble, they have Bumrah to bail them out, with Hardik and Arshdeep backing him,” Yuvraj said.He further highlighted the squad’s balance, noting the presence of quality spinners like Kuldeep Yadav even on the bench.Summing it up, Yuvraj reiterated: “Gambhir stuck to his process despite failures, and it rewarded him with the T20 World Cup.”
Article Summary: Former india all-rounder yuvraj singh has credited head coach gautam gambhir ’s unwavering belief in his methods as the key reason behind india’s t20 world cup triumph. Article Title: Stephen Curry: “I’m pulled in different areas”: Stephen Curry opens up about pressure in his family life as Ayesha Curry faces scrutiny
Article Text:
Stephen Curry opens up about pressure in his family life as Ayesha Curry faces scrutiny.(Image via Getty Images)

Stephen Curry shares an honest opinion about the pressure of responsibilities he faces in his personal life amid marriage to Ayesha Curry



Stephen Curry’s wife, Ayesha Curry, got brutally trolled for her controversial comments on marriage



Stephen Curry, the NBA legend, and his wife, Ayesha Curry, seem to have built a pretty beautiful life for themselves and their four young children.While the NBA legend Stephen Curry has always tried to keep his personal and professional life different, the four time NBA champion has been vocal about the pressure he faces in his life.A few weeks ago, in an interview with CNBC, the Golden State Warriors’ star player opened up about the pressure he is under at home as he said, "It's an idea of, 'Are you doing everything you can to take care of people that are relying on you?'"The NBA legend added, “Are you fulfilling your full potential in all different areas that I've set out to do? [...] We all like to be a better husband, a better father, more present at times, just because we're pulled — I'm pulled — in a lot of different areas…”Stephen Curry continued, “Those are daily commitments, daily thoughts that you have to weave through."Over the last few years, Stephen Curry’s high profile marriage to popular entrepreneur and chef, Ayesha Curry, has raised eyebrows.This has also led to immense trolling as Stephen Curry’s wife, Ayesha Curry, has continuously been trolled and shamed for her comments and opinions.A few months ago, Stephen Curry’s wife, Ayesha Curry, made an appearance on Alex Cooper’s popular podcast, Call Her Daddy, where she opened up about how she had different aspirations in life which were relatively different from marrying an NBA legend.In a heartfelt conversation, Ayesha Curry shared how she initially planned to have a career of her own and did not want to get married or have children.But her feelings changed when she fell deeply in love with the Golden State Warriors’ star player, Stephen Curry, when she was a young adult.The two got married young and have had four children since then.While Ayesha Curry’s statements seemed like an innocent confession, she was brutally trolled as fans accused her of wanting to divorce Stephen Curry.This led to both Stephen Curry and Ayesha Curry distancing themselves from the drama even as they continue to remain silent about it.
Article Summary: Stephen curry opens up about pressure in his family life as ayesha curry faces scrutiny.
